title: `--url` is interpolated into HTML without escaping, allowing script injection in `prompt`/`open`
reasoning: Both `prompt` and `open` build an `<iframe src="...">` wrapper by concatenating the raw `o.url` string directly into an HTML attribute. `assertUrlAllowed` only classifies the URL; it does not sanitize or normalize the original string before that same raw value is rendered. A URL containing a quote can break out of the `src` attribute and inject arbitrary HTML/JS into the wrapper document. In `prompt`, that injected code runs in the bridged page context because `withBridge(...)` is always applied and the default CSP permits inline scripts.
reproduction: Run `glimpse prompt --url 'http://localhost/" onload="alert(1)'` and the injected handler is rendered as part of the wrapper HTML instead of remaining confined to the iframe URL.
recommendation: Construct the iframe with an escaped/serialized URL value, e.g. by parsing once with `new URL(...)` and interpolating `url.href`, or by HTML-escaping attribute values before insertion. Add a regression test that passes quotes in `--url` and asserts the generated HTML keeps them inside the `src` value.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are present for the CLI entrypoint, and there is no evidence of a regression test covering hostile `--url` input.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CLI-level test for `prompt --url` and `open --url` that stubs the downstream call, passes a URL containing quotes, and asserts the generated HTML contains a safely escaped `src` attribute with no injected attributes or tags.

history.reasoning: The cited evidence location still exists in src/cli.ts lines 25-26, but prompt/open no longer concatenate raw o.url into the iframe HTML. They now call iframeForUrl(o.url), implemented in src/cli-helpers.ts to parse with new URL(rawUrl).href and HTML-escape &, quotes, apostrophes, <, and > before interpolation. A regression test exists at test/cli-helpers.test.ts and asserts a hostile onload payload is not emitted as an injected attribute. The built CLI artifact dist/cli.mjs also contains the same iframeForUrl/escapeHtmlAttribute logic, so the packaged bin is not stale for this issue. Targeted and full test suites pass.
